revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:荒田別]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Kamitsukeno no Taji|qid=Q11359475|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi}}; {{ill|Q108679309|qid=Q108679309|1=Utsufuruno-mikoto}}; {{ill|Takahase|qid=Q17209207|1=Takehase}}|father={{ill|Q17230461|qid=Q17230461|1=Mimorowake|2=ja|3=御諸別王|lt=Mimorowake|4=zh|5=御諸別王|qid=Q17230461}}}}
'''Aratawake''' (荒田別, also 大荒田別命) was a figure from Japan's {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, recorded in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is described as an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, a military commander serving {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (15th Emperor of Japan). == Historical Accounts == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the 49th year of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}’s reign (traditional date), Aratawake, along with Kagawawake, was appointed as a general and participated in the conquest of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}. It is recorded that they returned to Japan in the second month of her 50th year. In the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}’s reign, Aratawake was dispatched to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] alongside Hikoitsuse. The following year, they returned to Japan accompanied by the scholar {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|qid=Q482722|lt=Wani|1=Wani (scholar)}}. The record explicitly describes Aratawake and Hikoitsuse as "ancestors of the Kamitsukeno clan." According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Aratawake is listed as the father of {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|qq=}}. The Nihon Shoki further notes that Tamichi was a younger brother of {{ill|Takehase|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehase|qq=}}, who is also identified in genealogical records as a son of Aratawake. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} mentions Aratawake in relation to a petition by {{ill|Sugano no Mamichi|en|Sugano no Mamichi|qid=Q11619464|lt=Sugano no Michimichi|1=Sugano no Mamichi}} in the 9th year of the Enryaku era (790 CE), acknowledging his historical mission to Baekje. The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}} also include his name in their accounts of the conquest of Silla. == Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} attributes several clans as descendants of Aratawake: * '''Imperial Lineage:''' ** '''Right Capital Ōno no Ason''': Descended from Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto’s fourth-generation descendant, Ōaratawake no Mikoto. ** '''Right Capital Tanabe no Fuhito''': Descended from Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto’s fourth-generation descendant, Ōaratawake no Mikoto. ** '''Right Capital Sajino no Kimi''': Descended from the same lineage. ** '''Yamato Province Hirokitsuko''': Descended from the same lineage. Related to Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto's fourth-generation descendant, Ōaratawake no Mikoto. ** '''Kawachi Province Tomi no Muraji''': Related to Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto's descendant, Kamitsukeno no Tamichi. * '''Uncertain Lineage (Miscellaneous Surnames):''' ** '''Kawachi Province Ikei''': Descended from Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto’s fourth-generation descendant, Aratawake no Mikoto. == Analysis == Many clans listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of Aratawake were based in Kawachi Province, suggesting that Aratawake is a figure from the traditions of Kawachi’s scribe clans. Some theories link this to the migration of Kawachi clans to the Kamitsukeno region (modern Gunma Prefecture).<ref name="荒田別-氏族"/> The historical validity of Empress Jingū’s conquest of Silla is debated, and by extension, the historical existence of Aratawake is uncertain. However, some scholars suggest that early figures involved in diplomacy with Korea are plausible within the Kamitsukeno clan’s narrative.<ref>Mizoguchi Mutsuko, "Aratawake," Asahi Nihon Rekishi Jinmei Jiten.</ref> The name "Arata" has also been associated with the {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|qid=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}} in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} (present-day {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ōta, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}). Some theories propose that Aratawake may have been the interred individual in the massive {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qid=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}}, which dates to the late Kofun period.<ref>Maesawa Terumasa, *Kenokoku no Kenkyū: Kofun Jidai no Kaimei*, Gendai Shichōsha, 1982.</ref> == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=2010|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Pre-Asuka Period People|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Pre-Asuka Period People|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|荒田別|version=99874832|comment=Imported full ja history}}
